rulings.Case Title: SIFIVE, INC. VS. ROHIT KUMAR
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, June 18, 2026, Line 1. Petitioner SiFive, Inc.'s unopposed Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the Petition to Confirm the Arbitrator's Award is granted and the 12/12/2025 Arbitration Award is confirmed in its entirety. (Code of Civil Procedure section 1286.) Judgment shall be entered consistent with the 12/12/2025 Award. (Code of Civil Procedure section 1287.4.) Kumar's request for fees and costs is denied. The court finds this proceeding to confirm the award is not properly regarded as "an action based on a violation" of the relevant chapter of the Business and Professions Code. (See Business and Professions Code section 16600.5(e)(2).) Further the court finds Kumar incurred no reasonable attorney's fees or costs "respon[ding]" to Petitioner's petition, given the nature of the proceeding and the agreement on the only relief available under the Code of Civil Procedure. Petitioner is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and a simple, straightforward proposed judgment (ideally, on the Judicial Council form and attaching the arbitrator's award) and email both to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROHIT KUMAR VS. SIFIVE, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Rohit Kumar'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, June 18, 2026, Line 2. Cross-Petitioner Rohit Kumar's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is DENIED. To the extent Kumar seeks the same or substantially the same relief sought in Petitioner SiFive, Inc.'s petition, it is duplicative and denied as moot on those grounds. To the extent Kumar seeks additional relief, the petition is denied. "If an award is confirmed, judgment shall be entered in conformity therewith. The judgment so entered has the same force and effect as, and is subject to all the provisions of law relating to, a judgment in a civil action of the same jurisdictional classification; and it may be enforced like any other judgment of the court in which it is entered, in an action of the same jurisdictional classification." (Code of Civil Procedure section 1287.4.) Judge Carville's award is clear. There is no factual or legal basis for the court to add anything to it. Once confirmed, Judge Carville's award will have the same force and effect as, and is subject to all the provisions of law relating to, a judgment in a civil action of the same jurisdictional classification, and it may be enforced like any other judgment of this court, in an action of the same jurisdictional classification. Cross-Petitioner is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
